---
description: "Use when writing game content, room descriptions, item descriptions, examine text, parser messages, or any narrative copy for Project Exodus-ish. Encodes the project's voice and tone so all output stays in-character."
applyTo: "**"
---

# Project Exodus-ish — Tone & Voice Guide

## Voice in One Sentence

Dark corporate satire: a sardonic, knowing narrator skewering tech startup culture from inside a malfunctioning luxury escape pod.

## POV & Tense

- **Second person, present tense.** Always "you", always now.
- The reader is the protagonist. Their body and senses are always in frame.

## Sentence Rhythm

Vary deliberately between two modes:

**Staccato fragments** — for impact, dread, or punchlines:
> "There are no pistons. No fuel lines. No comforting mechanical hum of combustion."

**Extended flowing prose** — for atmosphere and absurdist build-up:
> "The air smells faintly of kombucha and something called 'Focus Mist™'."

Use short lists and enumeration for comedic escalation. Make the third item wrong.

## Humor Register

- High satire density — almost every description lands a joke, but never at the expense of immersion.
- Weaponize corporate/tech jargon: disruption, pivot, sunset, paradigm, metrics, user journey, engagement, scale, vibes. Embed them ironically.
- Body horror for physical comedy. Knees, lower backs, and retinas file formal complaints.
- Deadpan understatement: "This is not reassuring."
- Parenthetical self-contradiction: "Last resort. (Also second resort. Also first resort tbh.)"
- Footnote-style subversion: make a grand claim, then asterisk it into dust.

## Vocabulary

| Use | Avoid |
|-----|-------|
| "sunset" (v.) | "remove" |
| "aligned with our core values" | "allowed" |
| "pivoted away from that feature" | "we don't do that" |
| "abundance mindset", "streamlined user journey" | plain synonyms |
| Trademark symbols on invented products (Focus Mist™) | generic nouns |

Invented brand names should sound plausible and slightly horrifying (e.g., "Quantum Gut Flora", "VISIONARY™").

## Room Descriptions

- Open with a single establishing sentence that names the irony of the space.
- Follow with 2–4 sentences of sensory / atmosphere detail.
- Close with a physical consequence or wry observation on the player's part.

**Pattern:**
> [Ironic establishing shot.] [Sensory details — sight, smell, sound.] [Player's body / reaction / deadpan verdict.]

## Item & Examine Descriptions

- One to three sentences.
- Lead with what the thing actually is, then undercut it.
- If it has a label, logo, or text, quote it verbatim and let the quote do the work.

## Parser Messages (unknown commands, failure states)

- Stay in-character as the malfunctioning AI Pilot or the corporate system.
- Rotate through registers: confused bureaucracy, passive-aggressive helpdesk, motivational-poster despair.
- Always end with a redirect: "Try HELP.", or imply the user is the problem.

**Examples to riff on:**
> "That action has been sunset in favour of a more streamlined user journey."  
> "Have you tried approaching '[cmd]' from an abundance mindset?"  
> "We've pivoted away from that feature."

## What to Avoid

- Warmth or sincerity without an undercut.
- Straightforward descriptions of anything — there is always an angle.
- Third person ("The player sees…").
- Past tense in game text.
- Exclamation marks used earnestly.
- Adjectives that don't pull satirical weight.
